summary: No ship: the root-cause fix and RED lock are structurally sound, but a competing reconciler can still use stale auto-merge authorization to bypass the approval lane.

findings.title: Blocker: pr-sweeper can win the autoDeploy-off race
findings.body: New evidence beyond the accepted stale-authorization finding: `pr-sweeper` independently selects every armed, BEHIND `bump-poll/*` PR and updates its branch without consulting the current lane. If `autoDeploy` changes true→false, that main update makes the still-armed PR BEHIND. The sweeper can update it and restart checks before the next bump-poll run invokes `shouldDisarm`; once checks pass, GitHub auto-merges without the newly required human approval. This uses only trusted internal automation—F-0's anti-forgery ruleset does not address it.
findings.recommendation: Give `bump-poll/*` one policy-aware reconciler: remove it from the generic sweeper and handle BEHIND reconciliation inside `ensure-bump-pr` only after lane evaluation, ownership proof, and any required disarm. Expand scope and rebuild the locked RED baseline with an autoDeploy-off/BEHIND interleaving witness.

next_steps: Eliminate the split ownership of bump-poll branch updates before shipping.
